reasoning: Since no search tools are provided, I am predicting based on statistical properties of expected inflation as a time series and the provided empirical base rate. At 2.32%, 5-year expected inflation is slightly above the likely long-run anchor of ~2.0-2.2%, suggesting a slight tendency toward mean reversion (and thus decreasing). This gives a probability slightly under 0.50. For a monthly time series, changes are similar to a random walk with small mean-reversion drift. Therefore, I estimate a 45% chance of an increase in the 1-month horizon. As the forecast horizon extends to 3 months, accumulated variance dilutes the short-term drift, pulling the probability closer to 50% (48%).
